title: Political parties
description: Practice your skills in this quiz on how parties impact the electorate and government
topic_path: What roles do political parties play in U.S. politics?

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the graph and table below to answer the following question. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/aedd07fefbec8f7cfee2515c1731f823.png)

**Based on the data, how many years between 1995 until 2009 did the Republicans serve as the majority party in control of the Senate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Eight years
Six years
Sixteen years
Ten years

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements describes the role political parties within Congress play to promote their party’s public policy agenda? 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The majority party selects committee chairs who promote the party’s legislative priorities
The minority party recruits candidates for government office when there is a special election
The majority party appoints staffers in the executive branch, including the bureaucracy, who promote the party’s legislative priorities
The minority party controls the rules and calendar, ensuring they are favorable to their party’s policy agenda

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the table below to answer the following question. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/aedd07fefbec8f7cfee2515c1731f823.png)

**Looking at the chart from 1981 until 2011, which of the following is an accurate trend related to party control of the Senate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Republicans maintained the longest period of continuous control of the Senate during this time
The U.S. experienced a unified government during this time period with both the president and Congress representing the same party
The Democratic Party controlled the Senate most of the time during these years
Transitions in party control of the Senate occurred most often following mid-year elections

assessment_items.question_content:
**Linkage institutions are best defined as which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Groups that connect people to the government and turn people’s concerns into political issues
Organizations of people with similar political ideologies that seek to influence public policy by getting candidates elected
A formal or informal association of people seeking to influence governmental policy in favor of their interests
Organizations that raise money with the goal of supporting or defeating candidates, parties, or legislation

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is a linkage function primarily associated with interest groups? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Lobbying policy-makers
Presenting poll results
Endorsing party platforms
Interviewing citizens

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best explains how midterm elections serve an important linkage function with the electorate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They can determine whether the president and White House agenda will be replaced
They can directly affect the composition and holdings of the Supreme Court
They can reverse the constitutionality of executive orders and signing statements
They can alter the legislative agenda if the majority party changes hands in Congress

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios best illustrates the linkage function that the National Association of Realtors (NAR) would have with regard to a recently passed federal law protecting home buyers?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Sponsoring a new candidate and mobilizing voters to unseat the author of the legislation in the next election
Investigating and reporting possible corruption in the passage of home buyer protection legislation
Submitting an *amicus curiae* brief for the federal district court to review and overturn the recently passed law
Sharing research and constituent preferences for enforcing the law at a Housing and Urban Development (HUD) hearing

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios is an example of how the media can act as a linkage institution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A major newspaper organization publishes poll results indicating the percentage of the American public who support legislation to ban affirmative action programs
The head of a news network donates \$10,000 of his private funds to the Democratic candidate in the presidential election
A reporter publishes an article on the importance of using reusable straws over plastic straws
A news anchor posts a Twitter poll asking his followers to vote whether he should wear a green tie or a purple tie that day

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios is an example of how an interest group can act as a linkage institution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: March for Our Lives conducts a letter-writing campaign to key members of Congress to gain their support for gun control legislation
American Beverage Association hosts a wine tasting event for its new members
The AARP sends out subscription renewal notices that include membership benefits
The National Rifle Association sends out a list of candidates seeking to be elected to positions within the organization

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is a linkage function primarily associated with US political parties?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Influencing lawmakers to vote a certain way on specific political policy issues
Observing and communicating with the public on policy making and the performance of elected officials
Scheduling elections to determine candidates best suited to represent member interests
Mobilizing voters to elect a candidate advocating preferred ideological positions on political issues

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best explains the function of the platforms of the two major US political parties?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Platforms include important constitutional provisions in support of the two-party system in the United States
Platforms are intended to provide a particular political or economic ideology to appeal to a select set of voters in the electorate
Platforms are created on a yearly basis to address the specific political and economic issues by which voters can hold elected officials accountable
Platforms are a set of general principles, goals, and strategies designed to give voters a sense of what candidates believe in and will address if elected

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following important functions of democracy would most likely be more difficult without political parties?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Educating voters on upcoming elections
Raising funds for candidates’ campaigns
Reducing voter fraud in elections
Eliminating the patronage system in the bureaucracy

assessment_items.question_content:
Ahead of statewide elections, leaders from a political party meet to evaluate their strategy in competitive districts. They discuss party messaging and the types of candidates who might appeal to local voters. One leader emphasizes the importance of choosing candidates who reflect the district’s values and can raise funds effectively. Another suggests hosting events to raise the visibility of potential candidates and make the party’s stance on key issues clearer to the public.

**Which function of political parties is most directly reflected in the scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Recruiting people who can represent the party in future elections
Providing resources and support to help elected officials govern effectively
Organizing public events to increase voter turnout among the party base
Coordinating campaign communications like ads to boost name recognition

assessment_items.question_content:
A state-level political party launches a “Get Out the Vote” campaign aimed at increasing youth participation in elections. The campaign includes holding town halls on college campuses, using social media to explain registration procedures, and offering free transportation to polling places on Election Day.

**What primary function of political parties is reflected in this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Mobilizing and educating voters
Engaging in grassroots lobbying
Implementing public policy
Encouraging political participation through issue advocacy

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the primary power of political party leadership in the U.S. Congress?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Controlling committee assignments and influencing the legislative calendar
Appointing Supreme Court justices to interpret congressional laws
Regulating media coverage of legislative proceedings
Enforcing compliance with campaign finance laws among party members
